info.title: Onshape API - Part
info.description: Export Parts and access Part details.

tags.name: Part
tags.description: Export Parts and access Part details.

get.summary: Get all parts in a workspace, version, or microversion.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: elementId
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: withThumbnails
parameters.description: Whether or not to include thumbnails (not supported for microversion)

parameters.name: includePropertyDefaults
parameters.description: If true, include metadata schema property defaults in response

get.summary: Get all parts in an element.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: Document ID.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: One of w or v or m corresponding to whether a workspace or version or microversion was entered.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: Workspace (w), Version (v) or Microversion (m) ID.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: Element ID.

parameters.name: withThumbnails
parameters.description: Whether or not to include thumbnails (not supported for microversion)

parameters.name: includePropertyDefaults
parameters.description: If true, include metadata schema property defaults in response

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: Id of document that links to the document being accessed. This may provide additional access rights to the document. Allowed only with version (v) path parameter.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

parameters.name: rollbackBarIndex
parameters.description: Index specifying the location of the rollback bar when the call is evaluated. A -1 indicates that it should be at the end of the featurelist.

parameters.name: elementMicroversionId
parameters.description: A specific element microversion in which to evaluate the request.

parameters.name: includeGeometricData
parameters.description: Whether or not geometric data should be included in the response.

get.summary: Get a part's bounding box details.
Synchronously export a part to a glTF file.
get.description:
Creates a synchronous export of the part (with limited tessellation settings) to a glTF file. 
* Returns a 307 redirect from which to download the exported file. 
* Export is much faster than asynchronous endpoints at the expense of limited control on tessellation settings. 
* Use the [PartStudio/createPartStudioTranslation](#/PartStudio/createPartStudioTranslation) asynchronous export for greater control. 
 
See [API Guide: Synchronous Exports](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/translation/#synchronous-exports) for more details.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

parameters.name: rollbackBarIndex
parameters.description: Index specifying the location of the rollback bar when the call is evaluated. A -1 indicates that it should be at the end of the featurelist.

parameters.name: elementMicroversionId
parameters.description: A specific element microversion in which to evaluate the request.

get.description:
Parts must have density. The returned schema includes the same information as in the Onshape [Mass Properties Tool](https://cad.onshape.com/help/Content/massprops-ps.htm). 
When three values are returned: 
 * The first is the calculated value. 
 * The second is the minimum possible value, considering tolerance. 
 * The third is the maximum possible value, considering tolerance.

parameters.name: did
parameters.description: The id of the document in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: linkDocumentId
parameters.description: The id of the document through which the above document should be accessed; only applicable when accessing a version of the document. This allows a user who has access to document a to see data from document b, as long as document b has been linked to document a by a user who has permission to both.

parameters.name: wvm
parameters.description: Indicates which of workspace (w), version (v), or document microversion (m) id is specified below.

parameters.name: wvmid
parameters.description: The id of the workspace, version or document microversion in which the operation should be performed.

parameters.name: eid
parameters.description: The id of the element in which to perform the operation.

parameters.name: configuration
parameters.description: URL-encoded string of configuration values (separated by `;`). See the [Configurations API Guide](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/configs/) for details.

parameters.name: rollbackBarIndex
parameters.description: Index specifying the location of the rollback bar when the call is evaluated. A -1 indicates that it should be at the end of the featurelist.

parameters.name: elementMicroversionId
parameters.description: A specific element microversion in which to evaluate the request.

parameters.name: useMassPropertyOverrides
parameters.description: If true, use the user mass property overrides when calculated mass properties

get.summary: Synchronously export a part to a Parasolid file.
get.description:
Creates a synchronous export of the part (with limited tessellation settings) to a Parasolid file. 
* Returns a 307 redirect from which to download the exported file. 
* Export is much faster than asynchronous endpoints at the expense of limited control on tessellation settings. 
* Use the [PartStudio/createPartStudioTranslation](#/PartStudio/createPartStudioTranslation) asynchronous export for greater control. 
 
See [API Guide: Synchronous Exports](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/translation/#synchronous-exports) for more details.

get.summary: Get a part's sheet metal bend table.
Synchronously export a part to an STL file.
get.description:
Creates a synchronous export of the part (with limited tessellation settings) to an STL file. 
* Returns a 307 redirect from which to download the exported file. 
* Export is much faster than asynchronous endpoints at the expense of limited control on tessellation settings. 
* Use the [PartStudio/createPartStudioTranslation](#/PartStudio/createPartStudioTranslation) asynchronous export for greater control. 
 
See [API Guide: Synchronous Exports](https://onshape-public.github.io/docs/api-adv/translation/#synchronous-exports) for more details.

get.summary: Get a list of a part's tessellation edges.
get.description: Returns the coordinates (in meters) of each edge's endpoints.

additionalProperties.description:
`0: AUTOMATIC` Set automatically, like a part name | 
`1: MERGED` Merged from another Part Studio | `2: FEATURE` Custom feature | `3: UNCONFIGURED` | `4: CONFIGURED` | 
`5: STANDARD_CONTENT` | `6: DEFAULT` Applied from metadata property configuration | `7: COMPUTED` Non-overridden, non-configured, computed property | 
`8: COMPUTED_CONFIGURED` Property is computed in this configuration; may also be configured in other configurations 
`9: IMPORT` Imported properties are handled separately

propertySourceTypes.description:
`0: AUTOMATIC` Set automatically, like a part name | 
`1: MERGED` Merged from another Part Studio | `2: FEATURE` Custom feature | `3: UNCONFIGURED` | `4: CONFIGURED` | 
`5: STANDARD_CONTENT` | `6: DEFAULT` Applied from metadata property configuration | `7: COMPUTED` Non-overridden, non-configured, computed property | 
`8: COMPUTED_CONFIGURED` Property is computed in this configuration; may also be configured in other configurations 
`9: IMPORT` Imported properties are handled separately

BTShadedViewsInfo.description: Images are returned as base64-encoded strings.

BTMetadataStateType.description: The current state metadata values if applicable.

closed.description: If type == COMPOSITE, indicates whether it is open or closed.

items.description: If type == COMPOSITE, this is the id of a constituent that can be found in the 'bodies' array.

consumedByComposite.description: Indicates if there is a closed composite that consumes this body.

centroid.description: Centroid, center of gravity, center of mass

items.description: Centroid, center of gravity, center of mass

principalAxes.description: Vector coordinates of the principal axes. Use `BTVector3d-389` as the `btType`.

appearancePropertyNodeId.description: Identifies the application of the appearance. Faces that share a value were assigned an appearance together.

BTBoundingBox-1052.description: An axis-aligned bounding box indicated by two opposite corners.

importMicroversion.description: Microversion that resulted from the import.

BTMParameter-1.description: A list of parameter values for instantiation of the feature spec. Parameters are present for all defined parameters, even if not used in a specific instantiation.

scopes.OAuth2ReadPII: Application can read your profile information
scopes.OAuth2Write: Application can write to your documents
scopes.OAuth2Delete: Application can delete your documents and workspaces
scopes.OAuth2Purchase: Application can request purchases on your behalf
scopes.OAuth2Share: Application can share and unshare documents on your behalf
scopes.document.create: Atlas Application can create a document
scopes.document.edit: Atlas Application can edit a document
scopes.document.delete: Atlas Application can delete a document
scopes.document.read: Atlas Application can read a document
scopes.enterprise.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.read: Atlas Application can read from an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.edit: Atlas Application can edit an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.delete: Atlas Application can delete an enterprise
scopes.enterprise.member.create: Atlas Application can create an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.read: Atlas Application can read the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.edit: Atlas Application can edit the details of an enterprise member
scopes.enterprise.member.delete: Atlas Application can delete a member from an enterprise
scopes.webhook.create: Atlas Application can create a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.edit: Atlas Application can edit a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.read: Atlas Application can read a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.webhook.delete: Atlas Application can delete a webhook on behalf of the logged-in user
scopes.PLMIntegration: PLM automation can invoke limited operations

BasicAuth.description: Use Basic Authentication with API Keys (key as username and secret as password) to authenticate requests.
